name: Magnesium sulfate
executiveSummary: Treatment of magnesium deficiency. Hypocalcemia. Adjunct in severe acute asthma.
indications:
## Main indications

Treatment of magnesium deficiency. Hypocalcemia. Adjunct in severe acute asthma.

> Adjust according to institutional protocol and medical prescription.

adulto.dose: Torsades: 1–2 g IV over 15 min. Eclampsia: 4–6 g loading dose per protocol.

pediatrico.presentation: 25% ampoules: 250 mg magnesium sulfate/ml = 2 mEq magnesium/ml; 25% solution (compounded): 250 mg magnesium sulfate/ml = 2 mEq magnesium/ml 1 mEq Mg = 12 mg Mg
pediatrico.infusionRate: 15 to 20 min in medical emergency; otherwise 2 to 4 hours.
pediatrico.dose: Dose expressed as elemental magnesium Daily requirement 0-6 months: 50 mg; 6 months- 1 year: 70 mg; 1-3 years: 150 mg; 4-6 years: 200 mg; > 6 years: 250 mg. Symptomatic hypomagnesemia IV-IM: 0,8-1,6 mEq/kg/dose every 4-6 h, maximum dose: 16 mEq/dose. Asymptomatic hypomagnesemia IV: 0,2-0,5 mEq/kg/day, maximum dose: 8-16 mEq/day; PO: 0,8-1,6 mEq/kg/dose every 6 h. Intestinal absorption deficit PO: 20-60 mEq/day; adults 60-100 mEq/day. Adjunct in severe acute asthma: 25 - 50 mg magnesium sulfate/kg/dose, maximum dose: 2000 mg magnesium sulfate/dose. See CIME Electrolytes Bulletin.
pediatrico.notes: IV administration: Incompatible with calcium, bicarbonate, and phosphate.

neonatal.dose: 25–50 mg/kg slow IV per NICU protocol.

stability:
## General

- Compatible in 0.9% NaCl and 5% dextrose.

## Pediatric guide

- Discard once opened.
adverseEffects:
## Adverse effects

Hyporeflexia, flaccid paralysis, hypotension, heart block, CNS depression.
